title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Chief Inspector Hueston and the police chief discuss the investigation into the death of Mrs. Agglesden. Hueston notes that the crime scene appeared to be an amateurish attempt at a burglary, suggesting it may have been staged by Jack Agglesden to divert suspicion. A perplexing detail involves a five-pound note found on Jack that bore the name of Mrs. Bottley on the back; while Jack claimed the money was from his mother, Mr. Agglesden and Gwendolyn Vane asserted that Mrs. Agglesden had refused to give him money that morning. Hueston proposes a theory that an unknown person ("X") overheard an argument, took the money, and tricked Jack into a trap.

The police chief reviews the potential suspects present in the house that night: Jack Agglesden, Gwendolyn Vane, Hester Aigle, the Lindstrom woman, Leo Aigle, and Mary Darlant. Based on a timetable, the police identify a window of opportunity between 6:58 PM and 7:30 PM. They also consider Michael, a brother and automobile salesman, who possessed a key and had not been previously investigated. Hueston expresses a personal motive to solve the case because he believes Mrs. Agglesden was a good woman.

In a separate setting, Arthur Calgary (using the name Calgary) observes Moira Craig, the widow of Jack, at a cinema. The following morning, Calgary visits Moira at her home. Moira discusses the past incident involving Jack, noting that Jack died of pneumonia in prison. She reveals that she had been considering divorcing Jack because of his imprisonment and his lack of stability.
